a: Information that’s passed to an App Clip on launch.
An error that an App Clip activation payload returns.
Error codes that an App Clip activation payload returns.
The user denied location access, or the source of the App Clip invocation wasn’t from an NFC tag or visual code.
The provided URL doesn’t match the registered App Clip URL.
A string that identifies the activation payload’s error domain.
Create a lightweight, in-the-moment experience or demo version for your app that’s instantly available.

t: Associating your App Clip with your website
a: Enable the system to verify your App Clip to support invocations from your website and devices running iOS 16.3 or earlier.

t: Choosing the right functionality for your App Clip
a: Review frameworks available to App Clips and identify functionality that makes a great App Clip.
Review how people launch your App Clip with invocation URLs, default and demo links, and advanced App Clip experiences.
Add code to quickly confirm a person’s physical location while respecting their privacy.
Help users discover your App Clip by using an NFC-integrated or scan-only App Clip Code.

t: Creating App Clip Codes with App Store Connect
a: Select one or more advanced App Clip experiences in App Store Connect and create App Clip Codes for users to scan to launch your App Clip.

t: Creating App Clip Codes with the App Clip Code Generator
a: Use the App Clip Code Generator command-line tool to verify your code’s colors, get color suggestions, and create App Clip Codes.

t: Creating an App Clip with Xcode
a: Add an App Clip target to your Xcode project and share code between the App Clip and its corresponding full app.
Archive the full app for your App Clip, upload it to App Store Connect, and distribute it to testers or publish it on the App Store.
Enable your App Clip to schedule and receive notifications for a short or extended time period.

t: Encoding a URL in an App Clip Code
a: Choose an invocation URL for your App Clip Code that you can encode efficiently.

t: Fruta: Building a feature-rich app with SwiftUI
a: Create a shared codebase to build a multiplatform app that offers widgets and an App Clip.

t: Interacting with App Clip Codes in AR
a: Display content and provide services in an AR experience with App Clip Codes.

t: Launching another app’s App Clip from your app
a: Enable people to launch another app’s App Clip from your app with App Clip links and offer a rich preview of it with the Link Presentation framework.

t: Offering Live Activities with your App Clip
a: Add a widget extension to your App Clip target and use ActivityKit to display Live Activities on the Lock Screen and in the Dynamic Island.

t: Preparing multiple App Clip Codes for production
a: Prepare your App Clip Codes to send to a professional printing service.

t: Recommending your app to App Clip users
a: Display an overlay in your App Clip to recommend your app to users.
Add code to respond to invocations and offer a focused launch experience.

t: Sharing data between your App Clip and your full app
a: Use CloudKit, Sign in with Apple, shared user defaults or containers, and the keychain to offer a smooth transition from your App Clip to your app.

t: Supporting invocations from your website and the Messages app
a: Display a Smart App Banner and the App Clip card on your website that people tap to launch your App Clip, and add support for invocations from the Messages app.

t: Testing the launch experience of your App Clip
a: Debug App Clip invocations, test the launch experience, and verify the configuration of your released App Clip.
Checks whether an App Clip invocation happened at an expected physical location.
The user denied location access, or the source of the App Clip invocation wasn’t an NFC tag or visual code.
The provided URL doesn’t match the invocation URL you registered for the App Clip.
The URL of the link that launched the App Clip.
